question: Looking at the US population map, which statement below is the most accurate?

options.A: The population differences are impossible to determine from this visualization.
options.B: North Carolina and Delaware have similar populations.
options.C: All of the highlighted states have nearly identical population levels.
options.D: The population gap between North Carolina and Delaware might exceed that between Florida and Texas.

explanation: North Carolina (36.4M) and Delaware (53.0M) have a 16.6M population difference, while Florida (29.6M) and Texas (32.5M) have only a 2.9M difference. However, the categorical color encoding groups North Carolina and Delaware into the same color bin (30M+ range), making their large difference appear similar, while Florida and Texas appear more different due to different color bins.
